stories.headline: Iran War: Trump Says Blockade Having 'Incredible' Effect | Daybreak Europe 05/01/2026
stories.summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, President Donald Trump has doubled down on the US blockade of Iranian ports, saying it's having an "incredible" effect and Iran's "economy is

stories.headline: Iran war: What’s happening on day 63 as Trump signals possible attacks
stories.summary: Iran’s president calls the US siege 'intolerable' as Donald Trump says war may resume.

stories.headline: Trump mulls US troop cuts in Italy and Spain over Iran war row
stories.summary: Trump suggested his administration was also looking into reducing its presence in Spain and Italy over clashes with their leaders on the war in Iran. The comments come just one day after the US president made a similar proposal in Germany.

stories.headline: Trump threatens to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain – Europe live

stories.headline: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: Iran war may cause food shortages in Africa, world’s largest fertiliser firm says
stories.summary: Yara CEO warns of global auction that would leave poorest countries scrambling for supplies they can ill affordThe Iran war could have “dramatic consequences”, causing food shortages and price rises in some of Africa’s poorest and most vulnerable communities, the head of the world’s largest fertiliser company has said.Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Yara International, said world leaders needed to guard against soaring prices and sho

stories.headline: ‘Profoundly distressing’: record number of deaths in custody and police operations in NSW
stories.summary: There were 66 deaths in custody and police operations in 2025 – 18 more than the previous yearWarning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have diedThere were a record number of New South Wales deaths in custody and police operations last year, with almost a quarter of prisoner deaths recorded as self-harm, a coroner’s court report states.Of those who killed themselves, most were by hanging, despite millions being inves

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress reduces ex-President Bolsonaro's coup prison sentence
stories.summary: Lawmakers defied President Lula da Silva’s veto and moved to adopt a bill that would dramatically reduce former President Jair Bolsonaro’s 27-year prison sentence for plotting a coup following his loss in the October 2022 presidential elections.

stories.headline: ‘I feel sorry for people who have to deal with volatile gas prices. Our heat pump protects us’
stories.summary: The war on Iran has unexpectedly boosted the UK’s uptake of heat pumps. But are they really worth the investment?

stories.headline: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy’s push for EU membership strains ties with allies
stories.summary: Ukrainian president ‘had to hear some harsh truths’ from leaders at Cyprus summit

stories.headline: Ooredoo CEO: Zero Interruptions Due to Regional Crisis
stories.summary: Qatar telco Ooredoo has kicked off the year with steady growth, reporting 1Q revenue up 6% y/y, in line with analyst estimates while profit climbed 4.7%. Group CEO Aziz Aluthman Fakhroo spoke to Bloomberg’s Jennifer Zabasajja on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the numbers and growth strategy despite regional tensions. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yen’s Correlation With Oil Hit 2021 High Before Intervention
stories.summary: The correlation between dollar-yen and Brent crude rose to its highest level since late 2021 a day before Japanese authorities intervened in the currency market, underscoring the tighter link between oil prices and the yen.

stories.headline: ECB Increasingly Likely to Hike in Near Future, Muller Says
stories.summary: The European Central Bank will probably increase borrowing costs if the Iran war persists, according to outgoing Governing Council member Madis Muller.

stories.headline: Even Mainers Who Love Janet Mills Are Relieved to See Her Leave Race
stories.summary: Voters who watched Gov. Janet Mills struggle to gain traction in the Democratic Senate primary said they were unsurprised — and in many cases, ready — to see her exit.

stories.headline: Mills Exit Is a Blow to Schumer as Democrats Question His Strategy
stories.summary: Senator Chuck Schumer, the New York Democrat and minority leader, considered the Maine governor his top recruit for winning the majority. Critics said her collapse showed he is out of touch with the party’s voters.

stories.headline: The KOSPI's miracle surge comes with a warning label

stories.headline: Nagel Favors ECB June Hike Unless Outlook Improves Markedly
stories.summary: The European Central Bank will need to increase interest rates in June if there isn’t a significant change in the outlook, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

stories.headline: Yen Intervention Faces Headwind from Higher Energy Prices: 3-Minutes MLIV
stories.summary: Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Adam Linton break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yen Resumes Rally Sparked by First Intervention in Two Years
stories.summary: The yen resumed its advance in late afternoon trading in Tokyo after an earlier pause in the rally triggered by Japan’s first intervention in the currency market since 2024.

stories.headline: Ceres Power Shares Near 1,000% Gain as Fuel Cell Rally Extends
stories.summary: Ceres Power Holdings Plc extended a blistering rally this week, pushing one-year gains close to 1,000% as surging optimism around fuel cell technology drives the stock to multi-year highs.

stories.headline: Adani Group Unveils Restructuring as It Positions for Growth
stories.summary: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s conglomerate unveiled plans to overhaul its operating model, in an effort to cut decision-making time as it pushes for accelerated growth across businesses.

stories.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 5/1/2026
stories.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: New Banksy sculpture appears to show politician blinded by his own flag
stories.summary: Banksy has unveiled a new sculpture of a man stepping off a stone base with his face obscured by a flag.

stories.summary: Price of petrol in US jumps by nearly 30 cents in one week amid Strait of Hormuz blockade and Iran diplomatic deadlock.

stories.headline: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
stories.summary: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
stories.summary: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury quits amid row over participation of Russia

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Venice Biennale's international jury resigns as Italy's government opposes Russia's participation

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
stories.summary: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

stories.headline: Oil Holds Weekly Gain as Trump Says Iran Blockade Is Working

stories.headline: How Conservatives on the Supreme Court Weakened the Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The Supreme Court just overturned Louisiana’s congressional voting map, landing the latest blow to the landmark Voting Rights Act. Abbie VanSickle, a reporter covering the court for The New York Times, explains.

stories.headline: Hegseth Says Iran Cease-Fire Stops Clock for Congressional Approval
stories.summary: The defense secretary testified on the eve of the 60-day mark of the war, a major statutory deadline for the president to withdraw forces or seek approval from Congress to continue the fight.

stories.headline: Brazil’s congress approves bill reducing prison sentence of former president Jair Bolsonaro
stories.summary: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup.The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a sym

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.headline: 'Attacked 28 times in a day' - BBC visits heavily targeted US-UK base in Iraq

stories.headline: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel

stories.headline: Senators ban themselves from participating in prediction markets
stories.summary: The platforms, which have gained scrutiny recently, allow people to place bets on future events such as election results. The ban also applies to Senate staffers.

stories.headline: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital

stories.headline: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns

stories.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Futures Rise, Yen Pares Gain: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US equity futures were steady on Friday, suggesting a pause in the rally that’s pushed Wall Street gauges to record highs on strong megacap tech earnings. The yen resumed gains triggered by Japan’s intervention to support the currency.

stories.headline: Kenya’s Sawe hailed at State House after historic marathon
stories.summary: Kenyan runner Sabastian Sawe received a hero’s welcome from President William Ruto.

stories.headline: 'We are ready to discuss digital rules with the US but cannot wipe out rules,' EU trade chief says
stories.summary: After months of pressure from the US, EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič told Euronews that Brussels has agreed to open a “digital dialogue” with Washington. Still, US tariffs on EU steel and aluminium remain.

stories.headline: Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid
stories.summary: Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOsThe volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them.Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing

stories.headline: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

stories.headline: What the Supreme Court did to the Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: Southern lawmakers can now draw congressional maps to break up minority voters, as the conservative majority continues to roll back decades-old civil rights protections.

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: The sub-Arctic town pitching itself as Canada's gateway to Europe
stories.summary: Churchill, Manitoba, best known as the world's polar bear capital, has ambitions of making its deep-water port a year-round trade route.

stories.headline: Indians lost $2.5bn to digital fraud in 2025 - now its central bank is fighting back
stories.summary: The RBI is mulling measures to tackle rising cases of digital fraud in India - but will they work?

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: Madagascar detains French national over alleged plot to stir unrest
stories.summary: Malagasy prosecuters used a Whatsapp group as evidence for the detained individuals alleged crimes.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.
